notable_pick_trades: Traded 2026 2nd-round and 4th-round picks to Houston Texans for LT Laremy Tunsil. Acquired 6th-round pick from SF (Brian Robinson trade). No 2nd or 4th-round picks available.
roster_context: Coming off a disastrous 5-12 season after reaching the NFC Championship Game in 2024. Jayden Daniels was shut down with a recurring elbow injury (non-throwing arm) that ended his season early. New coordinators on both sides: David Blough (OC) and Daronte Jones (DC). Washington was aggressive in free agency, signing 9+ external free agents — primarily on defense — but whiffed on top WR targets (Pierce, Doubs). Still expected to sign Brandon Aiyuk on a prove-it deal once SF releases him. Defense ranked last in yardage allowed and 28th against the pass in 2025. Roster was gutted of aging veterans and rebuilt with younger, faster defensive pieces. Only 6 draft picks (missing rounds 2 and 4) limits flexibility.

needs.explanation: Washington ranked 28th against the pass in 2025. Released Marshon Lattimore (torn ACL, $18.5M cap savings). Lost Jonathan Jones and Noah Igbinoghene to free agency. Signed Amik Robertson (slot/outside versatility) but still need a true outside CB1. Trey Amos showed promise as a rookie before breaking his fibula in Week 10 — he's the long-term hope but unproven. Mike Sainristil has been inconsistent, ranked in the bottom quartile in yards per coverage snap. The Commanders' own podcast identified CB as a possibility at No. 7 overall.
needs.current_starter: Trey Amos (returning from broken fibula), Mike Sainristil, Amik Robertson (FA signing)
needs.depth: Dyami Brown re-signed, but CB depth is thin after releasing Lattimore and losing Jones/Igbinoghene. Robertson adds versatility but isn't an alpha outside corner.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 if top CB like Avieon Terrell is available, or Round 3

needs.explanation: Terry McLaurin is the only established receiver on the roster and missed 7 games in 2025 with injuries. Deebo Samuel left via free agency. Washington failed to sign Alec Pierce and Romeo Doubs. Signed depth pieces Dyami Brown and Van Jefferson but neither is a viable WR2. Luke McCaffrey showed flashes before a broken collarbone. Jaylin Lane averaged 14.7 air yards per target but only had 16 catches. The team is expected to sign Brandon Aiyuk on a prove-it deal, but even then they need another WR — Aiyuk hasn't played since 2024. Already scheduled a top-30 visit with WR Makai Lemon. 2026 may be McLaurin's final year in Washington due to his cap hit.
needs.current_starter: Terry McLaurin, Luke McCaffrey (returning from injury), Jaylin Lane
needs.depth: Dyami Brown, Van Jefferson, Treylon Burks (re-signed for depth) — none are reliable starters. Brandon Aiyuk expected but not yet signed.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 if top WR available, or Round 3

needs.explanation: Washington allowed 8.1 yards per pass attempt (3rd worst in NFL) and had only 8 interceptions (4th worst). Safety Quan Martin allowed the 5th-highest passer rating in coverage. Will Harris was an underwhelming free agent signing in 2025. Percy Butler and Tyler Owens proved best suited for special teams. Signed Nick Cross (2-year, $14M) which helps, but the safety group still lacks a true playmaker. New DC Daronte Jones utilizes three-safety looks with interchangeable pieces — this draft class has three potential first-round safeties (Downs, McNeil-Warren, Thieneman).
needs.current_starter: Quan Martin, Nick Cross (FA signing), Percy Butler
needs.depth: Tyler Owens, Will Harris — lack of ball production and playmaking ability across the group
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 — Caleb Downs is a popular mock pick at No. 7

needs.explanation: This was the biggest need entering free agency and was addressed aggressively: signed Odafe Oweh (4-year, $100M), K'Lavon Chaisson (1-year, $12M), Charles Omenihu (1-year, $7M), and re-signed Drake Jackson and Deatrich Wise Jr. Dorance Armstrong returns from a torn ACL (had 5.5 sacks in 6 games before injury). However, the pass rush was still inconsistent — Washington got pressure less than 20% of the time in 2025. Lost Von Miller (37, FA) and Jacob Martin (signed with Titans). Still need a long-term young edge rusher opposite Oweh, as Chaisson and Omenihu are one-year deals. Multiple mock drafts have Washington taking an EDGE at No. 7.
needs.current_starter: Odafe Oweh (FA signing), Dorance Armstrong (returning from torn ACL)
needs.depth: K'Lavon Chaisson, Charles Omenihu, Drake Jackson, Deatrich Wise Jr. — good depth but most are on short-term deals
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 if a top EDGE falls, or Round 3-5 for developmental piece

needs.explanation: Released starting center Tyler Biadasz, who signed with the Chargers for $30M. This leaves a significant hole at center. Nick Allegretti was extended and is the leading candidate to slide to center, but he has minimal NFL experience there (78 career snaps pre-2025). Chris Paul was re-signed at left guard on a 1-year deal. If Allegretti moves to center, the guard depth behind Paul and Brandon Coleman is unproven. The team missed on Tyler Linderbaum (signed with Raiders) and Connor McGovern (re-signed with Bills). May look at Lloyd Cushenberry or address in the draft.
needs.current_starter: Nick Allegretti (projected C), Chris Paul (LG), Sam Cosmi (RG)
needs.depth: Brandon Coleman, Malaesala Aumavae-Laulu — thin at center especially. Allegretti at center is the plan but unproven.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 3 or Day 3 for a developmental center

needs.explanation: Signed Leo Chenal (3-year, $24.75M) to add speed and blitzing ability — a major upgrade. Re-signed Bobby Wagner, who at 36 remains a leader but was a liability in coverage (allowed 3 TDs, 551 yards in coverage). Frankie Luvu is the anchor and should thrive under Daronte Jones. Jordan Magee provides rotational depth. Still need a long-term athletic LB who can cover — Wagner is a one-year bridge at best. Sonny Styles (Ohio State) and Arvell Reese are both mocked to Washington at No. 7.
needs.current_starter: Frankie Luvu, Bobby Wagner (re-signed), Leo Chenal (FA signing)
needs.depth: Jordan Magee, D.J. Johnson — Chenal addresses speed need but long-term off-ball LB who can cover is still a want
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 if Sonny Styles or Arvell Reese falls to 7, otherwise Round 3-5

needs.explanation: Re-signed Tim Settle (3-year, $24M) and retained Javon Kinlaw. Added Shy Tuttle. Lost Eddie Goldman (FA). Interior run defense was inconsistent and lacked a dominant interior pass rusher. The additions of Settle and the returning Kinlaw provide adequate starters, but depth and a 3-technique pass rushing threat would help the overall defensive rebuild.
needs.depth: Shy Tuttle, Ricky Barber — adequate depth but no dominant interior presence

needs.explanation: Jacory Croskey-Merritt showed flashes as a rookie 7th-round pick but was inconsistent. Chris Rodriguez left for Jacksonville (not tendered). Signed Rachaad White (1-year, $2-4M) as a pass-catching complement. Austin Ekeler, Chase Edmonds, Jeremy McNichols all departed. The backfield is functional but lacks a true feature back. Dan Quinn openly discussed taking a RB at No. 7 (Jeremiyah Love). Not a top-tier need given other holes, but could be addressed if BPA falls.
needs.depth: Thin after the top two — will need camp bodies at minimum
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 only if Love/top RB falls, otherwise Round 5-7

free_agency_impact: Signed EDGE Odafe Oweh (4-year, $100M, $68M GTD) — significantly reduces EDGE urgency from critical to important
Signed LB Leo Chenal (3-year, $24.75M) — adds speed and blitzing, partially addresses LB need
Signed CB Amik Robertson (2-year, $16M) — adds slot/outside versatility but not a CB1 solution
Signed DT Tim Settle (3-year, $24M) — solidifies DT rotation, reduces interior DL urgency
Signed S Nick Cross (2-year, $14M) — adds depth at safety but not a game-changing addition
Signed EDGE K'Lavon Chaisson (1-year, $12M) — rotational edge depth
Signed EDGE Charles Omenihu (1-year, $7M) — rotational edge depth
Signed TE Chig Okonkwo (3-year, $30M) — first offensive weapon added, move TE for Daniels
Signed RB Rachaad White (1-year, $2-4M) — pass-catching back, complements Croskey-Merritt
Re-signed Bobby Wagner — leadership and run defense, but coverage liability at 36
Re-signed LG Chris Paul (1-year) — solidifies starting left guard spot
Extended OT Laremy Tunsil — locks down LT for near future despite his age (32)
Extended IOL Nick Allegretti — projected to move to center after Biadasz release
Re-signed DE Deatrich Wise Jr. — rotational defensive end depth
Re-signed DE Drake Jackson — pass rush depth
Re-signed QB Marcus Mariota — backup QB
Re-signed WRs Dyami Brown, Van Jefferson, Treylon Burks — WR depth but no WR2
Released CB Marshon Lattimore — saved $18.5M, creates massive CB need
Released C Tyler Biadasz — creates center need, Allegretti is replacement plan
Lost Deebo Samuel (UFA, did not re-sign) — major loss at WR2
Lost Von Miller (UFA, 37 years old) — replaced by Oweh/Chaisson signings
Lost Jacob Martin (signed with Titans) — depth edge loss
Lost Chris Rodriguez (not tendered, signed with Jaguars) — replaced by White signing
Lost CB Jonathan Jones (UFA) — further depletes CB room
Failed to sign WR Alec Pierce and WR Romeo Doubs — WR remains critical need
Brandon Aiyuk expected to sign prove-it deal once 49ers release him — would help WR but not fully solve it
scheme_fit_notes: New DC Daronte Jones brings zone blitz concepts from his time under Brian Flores in Minnesota. He uses three-safety looks with interchangeable defenders and disguised coverages. This means Washington needs CBs who can play in zone and match coverage (not just man), and athletic safeties who can rotate between deep, box, and slot roles. Caleb Downs' versatility makes him a perfect scheme fit at No. 7. On offense, new OC David Blough runs a system similar to Detroit's, emphasizing play-action, a featured WR1 (McLaurin), and a dynamic slot receiver — hence the interest in Makai Lemon. The scheme values pass-catching RBs (White signing) and move TEs (Okonkwo signing). Edge rushers with length are preferred by Dan Quinn and Adam Peters, who noted they ideally want to 'err on the side of length' for EDGE prospects.
